content.text:
The test passed immediately. The existing implementation already correctly handles overpopulation — cells with more than 3 neighbors are filtered out (the filter keeps only `liveNeighbors === 2 || liveNeighbors === 3`).

This is a case where prior Green phase implementation went slightly beyond the strict minimum and already covers this behavior. The test verifies the existing behavior is correct.

Let me verify the test actually exercises the overpopulation case by confirming center (1,1) has 8 neighbors and was properly excluded.

- Expected value: result does NOT contain [1,1]
- Actual value: result excludes [1,1] because center has 8 neighbors (overpopulation), and current implementation filters out cells with neighbors > 3
- Diff: None - test passes
